Findings.Title: Block legacy authentication protocols
Findings.Detail: Legacy authentication blocked by 2 Conditional Access policy(ies).
Findings.Remediation: Create a Conditional Access policy: All users, All cloud apps, Client apps = Other clients, Grant = Block. Roll out in report-only first.

Findings.Title: Phishing-resistant MFA enforced for privileged roles
Findings.Detail: Phishing-resistant authentication strengths applied to privileged role(s): Modern MFA (Admins

Findings.Title: MFA registered for all enabled user accounts
Findings.Detail: MFA registration critically low at 46.1%. Enforcing AAD-3.1 CA MFA policy at this registration level will lock out 55 users.
Findings.CurrentValue: 46.1% registered (55 of 102 unregistered). Sample (first 10): admin@nrgtechservices.com, ASPNET@ndaco.org, BoardRoomA@ndaco.org, ChairMassageSignUp@nrgtechservices.com, copier@ndaco.org, CWAdmin@ndaco.org, cwsql@ndaco.org, dave.schimetz@ndaco.org, facilitymanager@nrgtechservices.com, G3211f8cc20bb4bcb929675285779bc4e@nrgtechservices.com
Findings.Remediation: Enable Registration Campaign (Entra ID > Authentication methods > Registration campaign). Use Temporary Access Pass (TAP) for bulk onboarding without helpdesk calls. Do not enforce AAD-3.1 until registration exceeds 95%.

Findings.Title: MFA enforced via Conditional Access (not per-user MFA or Security Defaults)
Findings.CurrentValue: Security Defaults: disabled — tenant managed via Conditional Access
Findings.RequiredValue: Security Defaults: disabled; MFA enforcement via Conditional Access

Findings.Title: Security Defaults disabled in favor of Conditional Access
Conditional Access: Legacy authentication blocked
Findings.CurrentValue: Enforced CA policy: 'All Users - Legacy Auth Clients - Block Access'
Findings.RequiredValue: Enabled CA policy blocking client app types: other, exchangeActiveSync

Findings.Title: Conditional Access: MFA required for all users on all cloud apps
Findings.Detail: No enabled CA policy requires MFA for All users on All cloud apps. Compromised credentials provide immediate account access.
Findings.CurrentValue: No MFA CA policy for all users
Findings.RequiredValue: Enabled CA policy: All users (exclude break-glass), All cloud apps, Grant = Require MFA
Findings.Remediation: Create CA policy: All users (exclude break-glass named location), All cloud apps, Grant = Require MFA. Deploy report-only first. Do not enforce until AAD-2.1 registration exceeds 95%.

Findings.Title: Conditional Access: MFA required for Azure management
Findings.Detail: No enabled CA policy enforces MFA for Azure management (Azure portal, CLI, PowerShell). App ID: 797f4846-ba00-4fd7-ba43-dac1f8f63013.
Findings.CurrentValue: No MFA CA policy for Azure management
Findings.RequiredValue: Enabled CA policy targeting Microsoft Azure Management app with MFA grant
Findings.Remediation: Add Microsoft Azure Management to existing all-users MFA CA policy, or create a dedicated policy. Covers Azure portal, Azure CLI, and Azure PowerShell.

Findings.Title: No Conditional Access policies remaining in report-only mode
Findings.Detail: Report-only policies provide zero enforcement. They generate logs but do not block or require anything.
Findings.CurrentValue: 2 report-only policy/policies: Microsoft-managed: Require phishing-resistant multifactor authentication for admins; CA-IDP-002-SignInRisk-MediumHigh-RequireMFA
Findings.RequiredValue: All security CA policies in enabled (enforced) state
Findings.Remediation: Review each report-only policy. Enforce with sufficient MFA registration, or document acceptance with rationale and target date.

Findings.Title: Conditional Access: Sign-in risk policy configured (Entra ID P2)
Findings.Detail: No CA policy found targeting sign-in risk levels. Entra ID Protection signals (impossible travel, malicious IP, atypical behavior) are generated but not acted upon.
Findings.RequiredValue: Enabled CA policy: All users, All cloud apps, Sign-in risk = Medium+High, Grant = Require MFA (High = block or compliant device)
Findings.Remediation: Requires Entra ID P2 (included in M365 Business Premium). Create sign-in risk CA policy: Medium risk = MFA step-up; High risk = block or MFA + compliant device.

Findings.Title: Conditional Access: User risk policy configured (Entra ID P2)
Findings.Detail: No CA policy targeting user risk. Leaked credential detection and persistent behavioral anomalies do not result in automated account action.
Findings.RequiredValue: Enabled CA policy: All users, All cloud apps, User risk = High, Grant = Block or require password change + MFA
Findings.Remediation: Requires Entra ID P2. Create user risk CA policy: High user risk = block sign-in or require secure password change + MFA.

Findings.Title: Authenticator app number matching enabled
Findings.Detail: Push notifications without number matching are vulnerable to MFA fatigue (T1621). Attacker spams approvals until user accepts.
Findings.CurrentValue: Number matching: unknown. Additional context: enabled
Findings.RequiredValue: Number matching: enabled; Additional context: enabled
Findings.Remediation: Entra ID > Authentication methods > Microsoft Authenticator > Configure. Enable Number matching AND Additional context. Zero-downtime change — takes effect on next sign-in prompt.

Findings.Title: Global Administrator count between 2 and 5
Findings.RequiredValue: Between 2 and 5 permanent Global Administrator assignments

Findings.Title: No on-premises synced accounts hold Entra ID admin roles
Findings.Detail: On-prem AD compromise (DCSync, credential dump, domain takeover) directly produces Entra Global Admin access via Entra Connect sync. This is a complete hybrid identity kill chain (T1078.002).
Findings.CurrentValue: 42 synced account(s) with Entra roles: dweiand@nrgtechservices.com [Global Administrator]; aschriock@nrgtechservices.com [Global Administrator]; CAnderson@nrgtechservices.com [Global Administrator]; cbraun@ndaco.org [Teams Communications Administrator]; Sync_ACO54NRGPUBLIC_98e1e0c63ff9@NRGTS.onmicrosoft.com [Directory Synchronization Accounts]; Sync_ACO54FILE_8456bac78169@NRGTS.onmicrosoft.com [Directory Synchronization Accounts]; Sync_ACO54NRGTERM_6e69e26d554c@NRGTS.onmicrosoft.com [Directory Synchronization Accounts]; Sync_ACO54DC5_bfa81503df8c@NRGTS.onmicrosoft.com [Directory Synchronization Accounts]; aschriock@nrgtechservices.com [SharePoint Administrator]; lbosch@nrgtechservices.com [SharePoint Administrator]; jmcdowell@nrgtechservices.com [User Administrator]; lbosch@nrgtechservices.com [User Administrator]; emcgurren@nrgtechservices.com [User Administrator]; aschriock@nrgtechservices.com [Service Support Administrator]; lbosch@nrgtechservices.com [Service Support Administrator]; aschriock@nrgtechservices.com [Teams Administrator]; cbraun@ndaco.org [Teams Administrator]; lbosch@nrgtechservices.com [Teams Administrator]; jmcdowell@nrgtechservices.com [Exchange Administrator]; dweiand@nrgtechservices.com [Exchange Administrator]; aschriock@nrgtechservices.com [Exchange Administrator]; lbosch@nrgtechservices.com [Exchange Administrator]; emcgurren@nrgtechservices.com [Exchange Administrator]; aschriock@nrgtechservices.com [Exchange Recipient Administrator]; aschriock@nrgtechservices.com [User Experience Success Manager]; jmcdowell@nrgtechservices.com [Security Administrator]; dweiand@nrgtechservices.com [Security Administrator]; emcgurren@nrgtechservices.com [Security Administrator]; CAnderson@nrgtechservices.com [Security Administrator]; aschriock@nrgtechservices.com [Compliance Administrator]; aschriock@nrgtechservices.com [Microsoft Hardware Warranty Administrator]; vlsclicensing@nrgtechservices.com [Global Reader]; aschriock@nrgtechservices.com [Global Reader]; jfricke@nrgtechservices.com [Global Reader]; ddriscoll@nrgtechservices.com [Global Reader]; lbosch@nrgtechservices.com [Global Reader]; dweiand@nrgtechservices.com [Billing Administrator]; jfricke@nrgtechservices.com [Billing Administrator]; dkraft@nrgtechservices.com [Billing Administrator]; aschriock@nrgtechservices.com [Helpdesk Administrator]; lbosch@nrgtechservices.com [Helpdesk Administrator]; dweiand@nrgtechservices.com [AI Administrator]
Findings.RequiredValue: Zero on-premises synced accounts in any Entra ID directory role
Findings.Remediation: Remove role assignments from synced accounts immediately. Create dedicated cloud-only admin accounts with no on-prem counterpart. On-prem accounts should be standard user accounts only in the cloud tenant.

Findings.Title: Global Administrator accounts are dedicated (no assigned licenses)
Findings.Detail: Licensed GA accounts are daily-use accounts — exposed to phishing, malware, and browser-based attacks during normal work. A compromised daily-use account with GA is an immediate full-tenant compromise.
Findings.CurrentValue: GA accounts with assigned licenses (likely daily-use): dweiand@nrgtechservices.com, aschriock@nrgtechservices.com, CAnderson@nrgtechservices.com
Findings.RequiredValue: All GA accounts unlicensed — admin tasks use dedicated cloud-only account, daily work uses separate licensed account
Findings.Remediation: Create dedicated cloud-only admin accounts: unlicensed, no mailbox, used only for admin tasks. Remove GA role from daily-use licensed accounts. Assign daily-use accounts appropriate scoped roles only.

Findings.Title: PIM used for Global Administrator role (JIT access model)
Findings.Detail: PIM is available but not consistently applied to the Global Administrator role. Permanent standing access means a compromised admin account immediately has full tenant privileges.
Findings.RequiredValue: All operational GA assignments via PIM eligible; zero or break-glass-only permanent assignments
Findings.Remediation: Migrate all operational GA assignments to PIM eligible. Activation settings: require MFA, justification required, max 8-hour duration. Consider approval workflow for production tenants.

Findings.Title: Break-glass Global Administrator account exists
Findings.Detail: No break-glass account candidates identified. All GA accounts have recent sign-ins and/or assigned licenses — inconsistent with a dedicated break-glass pattern. Without break-glass, a failed MFA provider or misconfigured CA policy can lock all admins out of the tenant permanently.
Findings.CurrentValue: No unlicensed cloud-only GA with stale or no interactive sign-in found
Findings.RequiredValue: 1-2 dedicated break-glass GA accounts: cloud-only, unlicensed, 20+ char random password, CA excluded, credentials sealed offline
Findings.Remediation: Create dedicated break-glass GA: cloud-only, unlicensed, 20+ char random password, excluded from MFA CA via named location, credentials printed and stored in physical safe. Configure sign-in alert in Azure Monitor. Test quarterly.

Findings.Title: Mailbox audit enabled tenant-wide
Findings.Detail: Tenant audit enabled. No mailboxes have AuditBypassEnabled.

Findings.Title: SMTP client authentication disabled tenant-wide
Findings.Detail: SMTP AUTH enabled tenant-wide. Approximately 69 of 69 mailboxes accept SMTP basic auth, bypassing MFA.
Findings.CurrentValue: Tenant SMTP AUTH enabled; 69 mailboxes affected
Findings.Remediation: Set-TransportConfig -SmtpClientAuthenticationDisabled $true. Verify per-mailbox: Get-CASMailbox | Where SmtpClientAuthenticationDisabled -ne $true

Findings.Title: POP3 disabled for all mailboxes
Findings.Detail: POP3 enabled on 69 of 69 mailboxes. POP3 uses basic authentication and cannot enforce MFA.
Findings.Remediation: Set-CASMailboxPlan -PopEnabled $false. For existing mailboxes: Get-CASMailbox | Set-CASMailbox -PopEnabled $false. Verify: Get-CASMailbox | Where PopEnabled | Measure-Object

Findings.Title: IMAP disabled for all mailboxes
Findings.Detail: IMAP enabled on 69 of 69 mailboxes. IMAP uses basic authentication and cannot enforce MFA.
Findings.Remediation: Set-CASMailboxPlan -ImapEnabled $false. For existing mailboxes: Get-CASMailbox | Set-CASMailbox -ImapEnabled $false. Verify count with: Get-CASMailbox | Where ImapEnabled | Measure-Object

Findings.Title: Customer Lockbox enabled
Findings.Detail: Customer Lockbox disabled. Microsoft support engineers can access tenant data without explicit approval.
Findings.Remediation: Microsoft 365 admin center > Org settings > Security & privacy > Customer Lockbox > Enable. Requires Microsoft 365 E3/E5 or equivalent licensing.

Findings.Title: Shared mailbox sign-in disabled
Findings.Detail: All 22 shared mailbox(es) have sign-in disabled.

Findings.Title: Modern authentication enabled for Exchange Online
Findings.Detail: Modern authentication (OAuth2) enabled for Exchange Online. Outlook and mail clients can use MFA.

Findings.Title: SPF record published: ndaco.org
Findings.Detail: ndaco.org has SPF with hard fail (-all).

Findings.Title: SPF record published: nrgtechservices.com
Findings.Detail: nrgtechservices.com has SPF with hard fail (-all).

Findings.Title: DKIM signing enabled: ndaco.org
Findings.Detail: Both DKIM selectors published for ndaco.org.

Findings.Title: DKIM signing enabled: nrgtechservices.com
Findings.Detail: Both DKIM selectors published for nrgtechservices.com.

Findings.Title: DMARC enforcement policy: ndaco.org
Findings.Detail: ndaco.org DMARC at p=quarantine. Move to p=reject after verifying clean mail stream.
Findings.CurrentValue: v=DMARC1; p=quarantine; rua=mailto:dmarc@nrgtechservices.com; ruf=mailto:dmarc@nrgtechservices.com; fo=1; sp=quarantine; adkim=r; aspf=r

Findings.Title: DMARC enforcement policy: nrgtechservices.com
Findings.Detail: nrgtechservices.com DMARC at p=reject - exact-domain spoofing prevented.
Findings.CurrentValue: v=DMARC1; p=reject; sp=reject; pct=100; adkim=s; aspf=s; fo=1; rua=mailto:dmarc@nrgtechservices.com; ruf=mailto:dmarc@nrgtechservices.com

Findings.Title: MTA-STS policy enforced: ndaco.org
Findings.Detail: ndaco.org MTA-STS policy state: System.Collections.Hashtable

Findings.Title: MTA-STS policy enforced: nrgtechservices.com
Findings.Detail: nrgtechservices.com MTA-STS policy state: System.Collections.Hashtable

Findings.Title: TLS-RPT configured for sending domains: ndaco.org
Findings.Detail: ndaco.org TLS-RPT configured. TLS delivery failure reports will be received.

Findings.Title: TLS-RPT configured for sending domains: nrgtechservices.com
Findings.Detail: nrgtechservices.com TLS-RPT configured. TLS delivery failure reports will be received.

Findings.Title: DNSSEC enabled for sending domains: ndaco.org
Findings.Detail: ndaco.org has DNSSEC enabled (DS records present). DNS records are cryptographically signed.

Findings.Title: DNSSEC enabled for sending domains: nrgtechservices.com
Findings.Detail: nrgtechservices.com has DNSSEC enabled (DS records present). DNS records are cryptographically signed.

Findings.Title: Anti-phishing policy with impersonation protection
Findings.Detail: Anti-phishing policy with impersonation protection active.
Findings.RequiredValue: Non-default policy with mailbox intelligence and domain impersonation enabled

Findings.Title: Safe Attachments policy with Block action
Findings.Detail: Safe Attachments Block policy exists but has no active rule applying it to recipients.
Findings.CurrentValue: Policy 'Standard Preset Security Policy1748356630442' Action=Block configured but no enabled rule
Findings.RequiredValue: Safe Attachments Block policy + active rule covering all recipients
Findings.Remediation: Create rule: New-SafeAttachmentRule -SafeAttachmentPolicy [name] -RecipientDomainIs [domain] -Priority 0

Findings.Title: Safe Links policy enabled tenant-wide
Findings.Detail: Safe Links policy configured but no active rule applies it to recipients.
Findings.CurrentValue: Policy 'Standard Preset Security Policy1748356631968' configured, no enabled rule found
Findings.RequiredValue: Safe Links policy + active rule covering all recipients
Findings.Remediation: Create rule: New-SafeLinksRule -SafeLinksPolicy [name] -RecipientDomainIs [domain] -Priority 0

Findings.Title: SharePoint tenant external sharing restricted
Findings.Detail: SharePoint tenant settings not accessible. Verify SharePointTenantSettings.Read.All permission is granted.

Findings.Title: Default sharing link type set to internal or specific people
Findings.Detail: SharePoint tenant settings not accessible. Verify SharePointTenantSettings.Read.All permission is granted.

Findings.Title: Anyone link expiration enforced (30 days or less)
Findings.Detail: SharePoint tenant settings not accessible. Verify SharePointTenantSettings.Read.All permission is granted.

Findings.Title: External user resharing of content disabled
Findings.Detail: SharePoint tenant settings not accessible. Verify SharePointTenantSettings.Read.All permission is granted.

Findings.Title: Unmanaged device access policy limits or blocks SharePoint access
Findings.Detail: SharePoint tenant settings not accessible. Verify SharePointTenantSettings.Read.All permission is granted.

Findings.Title: OneDrive default sharing link scoped to organisation
Findings.Detail: SharePoint tenant settings not accessible. Verify SharePointTenantSettings.Read.All permission is granted.

Findings.Title: Guest user resharing of items they do not own disabled
Findings.Detail: SharePoint tenant settings not accessible. Verify SharePointTenantSettings.Read.All permission is granted.

Findings.Title: Teams external access (federation) restricted
Findings.Detail: Teams federation is enabled with all external organisations. Any Teams user globally can contact your users and initiate chats or calls.
Findings.RequiredValue: Disabled, or AllowedDomains list with specific trusted organisations only
Findings.Remediation: Teams admin center > Users > External access > Set to Off or configure specific allowed domains.

Findings.Title: Teams consumer (personal account) federation disabled
Findings.Detail: Teams users can communicate with Teams personal (consumer) accounts. This allows unmanaged, unsecured personal accounts to interact with internal users and potentially exfiltrate data.
Findings.Remediation: Teams admin center > Users > External access > Disable "Allow users in my organization to communicate with Teams users whose accounts are not managed by an organization".

Findings.Title: Anonymous meeting join disabled
Findings.Detail: Unauthenticated anonymous users can join Teams meetings. Anyone with the meeting link can attend without signing in, with no audit trail.
Findings.Remediation: Teams admin center > Meetings > Meeting policies > Global > Disable "Anonymous users can join a meeting".

Findings.Title: Meeting lobby enabled for external and anonymous users
Findings.Detail: Meeting lobby is bypassed for everyone including anonymous users. Anyone with a meeting link joins directly without host approval.
Findings.RequiredValue: EveryoneInCompany or OrganizerOnly (requires lobby for external/anonymous users)
Findings.Remediation: Teams admin center > Meetings > Meeting policies > Global > Who can bypass the lobby > Change from "Everyone" to "People in my org".

Findings.Title: Meeting presenter role restricted to organiser or co-organisers
Findings.Detail: Anyone in a meeting can present by default. External attendees can share screens and content without restriction.
Findings.Remediation: Teams admin center > Meetings > Meeting policies > Global > Who can present > Set to "Organizers and co-organizers" or "Specific people".

Findings.Title: PSTN (dial-in) users bypass lobby disabled
Findings.Detail: PSTN (dial-in) users bypass the meeting lobby. Callers with the conference number and ID join directly without host approval.
Findings.Remediation: Teams admin center > Meetings > Meeting policies > Global > Disable "People dialing in can bypass the lobby".

Findings.Title: Third-party cloud storage apps disabled in Teams
Findings.Detail: Third-party cloud storage enabled in Teams: ShareFile, Box, GoogleDrive, DropBox. Users can share files from unmanaged external storage, bypassing DLP and retention policies.
Findings.CurrentValue: Enabled storage apps: ShareFile, Box, GoogleDrive, DropBox
Findings.Remediation: Teams admin center > Teams apps > Setup policies > Disable Box, Dropbox, Google Drive, ShareFile integrations.

Findings.Title: Email integration into Teams channels disabled
Findings.Detail: Teams channels accept email via a channel email address. Anyone who discovers or is given a channel email address can post content directly into Teams, bypassing message controls.
Findings.Remediation: Teams admin center > Teams settings > Email integration > Uncheck "Allow users to send emails to a channel email address".

Findings.Title: Unified audit log enabled
Findings.Detail: Purview collector did not run.

Findings.Title: Audit log retention configured (90 days minimum)
Findings.Detail: Purview collector did not run.

Findings.Title: DLP policy covers Exchange email
Findings.Detail: Purview collector did not run.

Findings.Title: DLP policy covers SharePoint and OneDrive
Findings.Detail: Purview collector did not run.

Findings.Title: DLP policy covers Microsoft Teams
Findings.Detail: Purview collector did not run.

Findings.Title: Retention policy for Exchange email configured
Findings.Detail: Purview collector did not run.

Findings.Title: Retention policy for SharePoint and OneDrive configured
Findings.Detail: Purview collector did not run.

Findings.Title: Sensitivity labels published to users
Findings.Detail: Purview collector did not run.

Findings.Title: Intune MDM authority configured and devices enrolled
Findings.Detail: Intune device management data not accessible. MDM may not be configured or the tenant does not have an Intune license.
Findings.Remediation: Microsoft 365 Business Premium, E3, or standalone Intune license required. Configure MDM authority in Endpoint Manager admin center.

Findings.Title: Windows device compliance policy requires BitLocker encryption
Findings.RequiredValue: Windows compliance policy with bitLockerEnabled = true

Findings.Title: Windows device compliance policy requires antivirus and firewall
Findings.Detail: No Windows compliance policy requires antivirus or firewall. Endpoints can be enrolled as "compliant" with no AV or firewall active.
Findings.RequiredValue: Antivirus = Required, Firewall = Required in Windows compliance policy
Findings.Remediation: Endpoint Manager > Devices > Compliance policies > [Windows policy] > System security: Set Firewall = Required, Antivirus = Required, Real-time protection = Required.

Findings.Title: iOS App Protection (MAM) policy configured
Findings.Detail: No iOS App Protection policy configured. Corporate data accessed via Outlook, Teams, or OneDrive on personal iOS devices has no copy/paste, screenshot, or backup restrictions. If a device is lost or an employee leaves, data cannot be remotely wiped.
Findings.RequiredValue: iOS App Protection policy covering Outlook, Teams, OneDrive at minimum
Findings.Remediation: Endpoint Manager > Apps > App protection policies > Create policy (iOS/iPadOS). Target Outlook, Teams, OneDrive. Set PIN, copy/paste restrictions, and remote wipe capability.

Findings.Title: Android App Protection (MAM) policy configured
Findings.Detail: No Android App Protection policy configured. Corporate data on Android personal devices has no controls. Data can be copied to personal apps, screenshots can be taken, and data cannot be remotely wiped.
Findings.RequiredValue: Android App Protection policy covering Outlook, Teams, OneDrive at minimum
Findings.Remediation: Endpoint Manager > Apps > App protection policies > Create policy (Android). Target Outlook, Teams, OneDrive. Set PIN, copy/paste, screenshot restrictions.

Findings.Title: Windows Hello for Business policy configured in Intune
Findings.Detail: No Windows Hello for Business policy found. Windows sign-in defaults to password only on enrolled devices. WHfB provides phishing-resistant biometric/PIN authentication at the device level.
Findings.RequiredValue: Windows Hello for Business enrollment policy enabled in Intune
Findings.Remediation: Endpoint Manager > Devices > Enrollment > Windows Hello for Business. Enable and require PIN with biometric option. Or create Identity Protection profile under Device configuration.

Findings.Title: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint connector enabled in Intune
Findings.Detail: No Microsoft Defender for Endpoint connector found in Intune. MDE threat risk scores are not integrated with device compliance, meaning compromised devices remain "compliant" in Intune.
Findings.RequiredValue: MDE connector configured and enabled in Intune
Findings.Remediation: Requires Microsoft Defender for Endpoint (P1 or P2) and Intune. Endpoint Manager > Endpoint security > Microsoft Defender for Endpoint > Open MDE portal and enable Intune connection.

Findings.Title: Enrollment restrictions configured beyond default allow-all policy
Findings.Detail: No custom enrollment restrictions found. The default policy allows all device types (Android, iOS, Windows, macOS) to enroll without restriction. Personal/unmanaged devices can enroll without controls.
Findings.CurrentValue: Default enrollment restrictions only (allow all platforms)
Findings.RequiredValue: Enrollment restrictions limiting allowed platforms and requiring corporate ownership
Findings.Remediation: Endpoint Manager > Devices > Enrollment restrictions > Create restriction. Block platforms not used in your org. Consider requiring corporate-owned device type.

Findings.Title: Default environment DLP policy configured
Findings.Detail: Power Platform DLP data not available. Install Microsoft.PowerApps.Administration.PowerShell for full DLP assessment: Install-Module Microsoft.PowerApps.Administration.PowerShell

Findings.Title: Power Platform tenant isolation enabled
Findings.Detail: Tenant isolation data not available via Graph beta API. Verify Power Platform is provisioned in this tenant.

Findings.Title: High-risk connectors blocked in Power Platform DLP policies
Findings.Detail: Power Platform DLP data not available. Install Microsoft.PowerApps.Administration.PowerShell for DLP connector assessment.

Findings.Title: Power Platform admin role assignments minimal
Findings.CurrentValue: No dedicated Power Platform admin role assignments (Global Admins have access implicitly)
Findings.RequiredValue: Minimal Power Platform Administrator role assignments

Findings.Title: Non-default Power Platform environments covered by DLP policy
Findings.Detail: Power Platform DLP data not available. Install Microsoft.PowerApps.Administration.PowerShell.
